channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: TikTok ban on hold for 75 days, but Trump may make a deal with China
item.description: President Donald Trump signed an executive order on Monday seeking to delay by 75 days the enforcement of a ban of popular short-video app TikTok that was slated to be shuttered on January 19. While signing the order, Trump suggested the United States government should be a half owner of TikTok’s U.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Cloud repatriation – how to balance repatriation effectively and securely
item.description: Public cloud is set to increase in popularity, however some are moving to private cloud. Here are the pros and cons of both.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Sorry America! A VPN might not be a quick solution to your TikTok withdrawal after all
item.description: Experts recorded VPN spikes of up to 1566% within minutes of the block being enforced. Here's why it wasn't enough to bypass the US TikTok ban.
item.dc:creator: chiara.castro@futurenet.com (Chiara Castro), TechRadar

item.title: Streamline automation procurement with Red Hat and AWS Marketplace
item.description: In today’s fast-paced digital landscape, organizations are under constant pressure to adopt new technologies quickly, manage costs effectively, and maintain robust security and compliance standards.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Trump repeals Biden’s AI oversight order, shifts focus to innovation-driven policies
item.description: On his first day as the US President, Donald Trump repealed a 2023 executive order by Joe Biden that mandated stricter oversight of artificial intelligence (AI) development.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Las empresas son más conscientes de la huella medioambiental de la IA generativa, pero tienen problemas para abordarla
item.description: La inteligencia artificial (IA) generativa tiene un creciente y significativo impacto negativo en el medioambiente. A pesar de ello, muchas organizaciones no están haciendo un seguimiento adecuado de la huella que generan, lo que está poniendo en peligro sus objetivos ESG.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: The Atom-Splitting Debacle: Trump's Inauguration Claim Sparks Global Reaction
item.description: US President Donald Trump's inauguration address sparks controversy with claims of American atomic splitting, but New Zealanders point to Ernest Rutherford's pioneering work in nuclear physics.
item.dc:creator: Charlotte Graham-Mclay, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump orders government not to infringe on Americans’ speech, calls for censorship investigation
item.description: President Donald Trump has ordered that no federal officer, employee or agent may unconstitutionally abridge the free speech of any American citizen.
item.dc:creator: Ali Swenson, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Army testing approach to ease communication with allies
item.description: Col. Heath Giesecke, the director of the Army’s Enterprise Cloud Management Agency, said a cloud-based commercial app is helping close a communication gap. The post Army testing approach to ease communication with allies first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Cloud infrastructure spending more than doubles in the third quarter of 2024
item.description: Spending on compute and storage infrastructure for cloud deployments has surged to unprecedented heights, with 115.3% year-over-year increase in the third quarter of 2024.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Trump's inauguration will usher in a crypto-friendly administration, and with it, new state policies
item.description: HARRISBURG, Pa. (AP) — The bitcoin-friendly administration of President-elect Donald Trump and an expanding lobbying effort in statehouses could push states to become more open to crypto and lead public pension funds and treasuries to buy into it.
item.dc:creator: MARC LEVY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Man admits to setting fire to building housing Wisconsin rep's office over TikTok ban
item.description: A man has admitted to setting fire to a building housing a House member from Wisconsin’s office over “recent talks of a TikTok ban,” according to local police.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: From backing a ban to being hailed as a savior: Inside Trump's TikTok shift
item.description: During his first term as president, Donald Trump led the effort to ban TikTok, the hugely popular video-sharing site he said posed threats to U.S. national security.
item.dc:creator: Jill Colvin And Barbara Ortutay, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Secure AI? Dream on, says AI red team
item.description: The group responsible for red teaming of over 100 generative AI products at Microsoft has concluded that the work of building safe and secure AI systems will never be complete.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: For TikTok users, mourning, frustration and clinging to hope as TikTok ban looms
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The U.S. is inching closer and closer to a potential TikTok ban — with the nation's highest court upholding a law that's set to halt new downloads of the app starting Sunday.
item.dc:creator: WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS and MAE ANDERSON, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Supreme Court upholds TikTok ban. No one knows what comes next
item.description: In a unanimous ruling, the Supreme Court voted Friday to uphold a law that will require TikTok to shut down as soon as Sunday, unless its Chinese parent company, ByteDance, agrees to sell the app. The court agreed with the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Issie Lapowsky, Fast Company

item.title: TikTok refugees are pouring to Xiaohongshu. Here's what you need to know about the RedNote app
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — As the fate of TikTok hangs in the balance, U.S. TikTok users are flocking to the Chinese social media app Xiaohongshu, also called RedNote – making it the top downloaded app in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: FU TING and DAVID COHEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Elon Musk’s Starship rocket explodes during SpaceX flight test
item.description: A SpaceX Starship rocket broke up in space minutes after launching from Texas on Thursday, forcing airline flights over the Gulf of Mexico to alter course to avoid falling debris and setting back Elon Musk’s flagship rocket program.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Who could buy TikTok? Only a few parties are serious about offering
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The supremely popular TikTok could be banned on Jan. 19 under a federal law that forces the video sharing platform to divest itself from its China-based parent company, ByteDance, or shut down its U.S. operations.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: How to save your TikTok videos, data ahead of looming ban
item.description: Barring a last-minute turnaround, the popular social media app will be unavailable in all 50 states Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Noem calls for ‘smaller, more nimble’ CISA during nomination hearing
item.description: South Dakota Gov. Kristi Noem, Trump's pick to lead DHS, criticized CISA's work on misinformation and said the agency "has gotten far off mission." The post Noem calls for ‘smaller, more nimble’ CISA during nomination hearing first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: Bitcoin soars past $100,000 ahead of possible early action on crypto by Trump
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The price of bitcoin topped $100,000 again early Friday as a pumped up cryptocurrency industry expects early action by Donald Trump when he's sworn in as president next week.
item.dc:creator: ALAN SUDERMAN, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Best ways to give your old iPhone a second life
item.description: Tech guru Kurt "CyberGuy" Knutsson explores the many different ways you can repurpose that old iPhone of yours rather than trashing it.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: What everyone’s missing about Android upgrades in 2025
item.description: When we talk about Android upgrades, it’s all too easy to miss the forest for the trees. It’s a familiar tale here in the land o’ Googley matters — and with some wacky-seeming changes on the way for Android upgrades in 2025, the myopic musings are only getting louder.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Python eats the world
item.description: In this week’s Python Report: Everyone’s favorite snake-named language is getting closer to being everyone’s favorite language, period. We also have a guide to unpacking the power of Python’s abstract base classes, and a tutorial for developers who want to use Cython to speed up their Python code.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Bill O'Reilly: Joe Biden going out same way as Aaron Rodgers
item.description: Bill O'Reilly believes outgoing president Joe Biden is "going out the same way" as New York Jets’ starting quarterback Aaron Rodgers.
item.dc:creator: Chris Cuomo, NewsNation

item.title: Biden's big semiconductor law will ramp up US chip production -- but at a high cost, report finds
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A sweeping 2022 law, touted by President Joe Biden as a way to revive U.S. manufacturing of semiconductors and reduce the country’s reliance on foreign-made computer chips, will “sharply increase production’’ of semiconductors in the United States.
item.dc:creator: PAUL WISEMAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Microsoft creates new internal unit for AI development
item.description: Microsoft is creating a new engineering group focused on artificial intelligence: Core AI — Platform and Tools. The group combines the company’s Developer Division with the AI Platforms work teams and some employees who previously reported to Microsoft’s CTO.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: AI health tech will transform your fitness routine
item.description: Stay up to date on the latest AI technology advancements and learn about the challenges and opportunities AI presents now and for the future.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Microsoft adds ‘pay-as-you-go’ access for AI agents with M365 Copilot Chat
item.description: Microsoft has added a “pay-as-you-go” option to access AI agents alongside its free M365 Copilot Chat plan.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Mac malware mayhem as 100 million Apple users at risk of having personal data stolen
item.description: New Mac malware variant targets browser credentials and personal data, researchers find. Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson gives tips to safeguard your Mac from the latest malware threats.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden signs ambitious order to bolster energy resources for AI data centers
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — President Joe Biden on Tuesday signed an ambitious executive order on artificial intelligence that seeks to ensure the infrastructure needed for advanced AI operations, such as large-scale data centers and new clean power facilities, can be built quickly and at scale in the United
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ericsson unveils genAI assistant for 5G network operations
item.description: Telecommunications and networking provider Ericsson recently launched its generative AI-based virtual assistant that uses large language model (LLM) technology to read, understand, and generate new content to provide personalized answers for network operators configuring wireless 5G networks, troubl
item.dc:creator: Network World
